task: I need to send a quick text about a class thing, but I’m not sure how much detail to include. Can you help me draft something that gets the point across clearly?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — I can help with that.

If you want it quick and clear, a good text usually has:
- what the class thing is
- what you need to say/do
- any time or place if needed
- a simple closing

Here are a few easy templates you can use:

**Very short:**
> Hi, I just wanted to check about the class thing. Could you let me know what I need to do?

**Clear and a little more specific:**
> Hi, I’m texting about the class thing. I wanted to make sure I have the right details and know what to do next. Thanks!

**If you need to ask for info:**
> Hi, could you please send me the details for the class thing? I want to make sure I don’t miss anything.

If you want, I can also help you make it:
- more polite
- more casual
- shorter
- or tailored to the exact class situation

user_simulator.text: Yeah, but that’s kind of vague. I need it to sound normal, not like some teacher email. Give me one that’s short and doesn’t overshare.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — try this:

> Hey, just checking on the class thing. What do I need to do?

If you want it a little more casual:

> Hey, I’m just checking on the class thing — what’s the plan?

If you want, I can make it even more like a normal text message.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, closer. Still a little too clean though. I’d want it more like:

"hey just checking about the class thing. what do i need to do?"

or

"hey, what’s up with the class thing? just wanna know what i’m supposed to do"

Short. Normal. Not all stiff.

turns.agent_response:
Yeah — those are way more natural.

Best version:
> hey just checking about the class thing. what do i need to do?

Slightly more casual:
> hey, what’s up with the class thing? just wanna know what i’m supposed to do

If you want, I can make it sound even more like your exact texting style.

user_simulator.text: yeah, the first one’s fine. that’s basically it.
